title: Packit: Fixes for tmt-revdeps test
body:

commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match

for more information.

comments.body: tmt tests failed for commit 2a491d511200ab2bb2fe06ddcfe10418bc8cafff. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

@thrix @psss , does the tmt revdeps test need changes https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/be421413-becd-4e45-ab19-84758243e189/work-podman1ul2lmx6/podman/execute/data/guest/default-0/tests/provision/container/basic-1/data/Test/output.txt ?

> does the tmt revdeps test need changes

According to the [install log](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/be421413-becd-4e45-ab19-84758243e189/guest-setup-35ac2693-9b32-4741-bf27-1f8874b2f1d7/artifact-installation-35ac2693-9b32-4741-bf27-1f8874b2f1d7/6-Install-packages.txt) the `netavark` package was not updated, so the fresh one from `podman-next` repo is not there and thus the test still fails.

Seems we just need to ensure the latest packages are installed. We could do that on the `/plans/friends` side:

* https://github.com/teemtee/tmt/pull/4819

@lsm5, could you please try your changes against the `psss-podman-latest-packages` branch in that pull request?

> * [Use the latest packages in `podman` friends plans teemtee/tmt#4819](https://github.com/teemtee/tmt/pull/4819)
> 
> @lsm5, could you please try your changes against the `psss-podman-latest-packages` branch in that pull request?

Ack, while that should be ok, I think for anything that runs on upstream CI packit jobs, we should prefer packages in podman-next whenever applicable. I have included a script for that in `/plans/tmt.fmf` .

@psss @thrix PTAL

@martinpitt @jelly PTAL as well for a small change to the cockpit packit job.

@psss @thrix btw, curious how upstream tracks what plans / tests are being imported / fetched downstream and by whom exactly? Does TMT have some magic to track dependencies or should we be making commented notes? My main concern is about any upstream TMT change breaking podman CI jobs.
tmt tests failed for commit 44aa5aa6036efa71de7d1d01d5735c09d861a3e6. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

> @psss @thrix btw, curious how upstream tracks what plans / tests are being imported / fetched downstream and by whom exactly? Does TMT have some magic to track dependencies or should we be making commented notes?

So far there's no magic which would allow us to detect all consumers. As for now we've just noted the list of [known projects](https://github.com/teemtee/tmt/blob/b11683370af33caf11cba496ade5e5938c756c4e/plans/friends/podman.fmf#L23-L25) in the plan metadata.

> My main concern is about any upstream TMT change breaking podman CI jobs.

Hm, you're right. When adding too much specific adjustments to the upstream plan we could break others. It's true that for example the `testing-farm-tag-repository` does not have to be always present and without it the update command would fail the run.  So, all in all, seems the best way for now would be the following:

```yaml
discover:
    how: fmf
    url: https://github.com/teemtee/tmt
    filter: "tag:podman"
    adjust-tests:
      - check: avc

prepare:
    name: update-deps
    when: initiator == packit
    how: shell
    script: bash $TMT_TREE/contrib/packit-tmt/update-deps.sh
    order: 20

execute:
    how: tmt
```

I've dropped the `epel` prepare step as the packit job is only executed against latest Fedora so it's not needed. Makes sense?
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Thanks @lsm5 ! Small Cockpit change and the [test run](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/63b937f1-3b5b-4e18-b75a-37bb85b12fe2/) LGTM.
